Test Cases Executed.Test Steps:

Test Cases Executed.Test Steps:

2. Wait 10 minutes (or configured interval).

Test Cases Executed.Test Steps:

2. Select a backup file.

Test Cases Executed.Test Steps:
1. Set retention policy to 7 days.
2. Manually trigger cleanup or wait for scheduled job.
3. Verify backups older than 7 days are deleted.
Test Cases Executed.Expected Result: Files older than 7 days are removed from backup directory.

Defects Identified.Assigned To: [to be filled out by system's developers]
Defects Identified.Date Fixed: [to be filled out by system's developers]

Severity Levels Guide.Description: Issues that completely block or "stop" the normal functioning of the system. The software is unusable or a significant part of it is not functioning.
Severity Levels Guide.When to Use: Use this level when the defect prevents further testing or the software cannot be used at all.
Severity Levels Guide.Guidelines: Reserved for issues that halt all testing or prevent essential functions.
Severity Levels Guide.Example: Users are unable to log in to the email system

Severity Levels Guide.Description: Issues that have a significant impact on the functionality, causing major disruptions or impairing the core features.
Severity Levels Guide.When to Use: Use for defects that have a serious impact on the system but do not completely block its use.
Severity Levels Guide.Guidelines: Used for severe issues that significantly impact the system but do not completely halt it.
Severity Levels Guide.Example: Emails sent from the system are not reaching the recipients.

Severity Levels Guide.Description: Issues that have a noticeable impact but don't critically affect core functionalities. They may affect non-essential features or have workarounds available.
Severity Levels Guide.When to Use: Use for defects that are important but not critical to the overall system.
Severity Levels Guide.Guidelines: Applied to important issues that need attention but don't critically affect core functionalities.
Severity Levels Guide.Example: Attachments in emails take longer than expected to upload.

Severity Levels Guide.Description: Issues that have a minor impact on the software. These defects are generally cosmetic or have little effect on system functionality.
Severity Levels Guide.When to Use: Use for issues that are of low priority, such as minor visual imperfections or inconveniences.
Severity Levels Guide.Guidelines: For less critical issues that can be addressed after higher severity defects.
Severity Levels Guide.Example: The spell-check feature is not working in the email composer.

Severity Levels Guide.Description: Minor issues that do not affect functionality and have little to no impact on the user experience.
Severity Levels Guide.When to Use: Use for purely aesthetic issues or other trivial matters that do not impact the software's operation.
Severity Levels Guide.Guidelines: For issues that are minimal and have no significant impact on functionality.
Severity Levels Guide.Example: The color of the "Compose" button is slightly different from the design specifications.
